subject: Chemistry
title: Minamata Disease: Mercury Poisoning
description: A short lesson to help you apply Minamata Disease: Mercury Poisoning.

hook.text: In the 1950s, cats in Minamata, Japan, went mad and threw themselves into the sea. Then people started dying.

buildup.text: The Chisso chemical factory dumped methylmercury waste into Minamata Bay for decades. Fish absorbed the mercury.

discovery.text: Mercury biomagnified up the food chain. People who ate the fish developed nerve damage, blindness, and paralysis.

twist.text: Chisso knew mercury was the cause by 1959 but continued dumping until 1968. They paid to suppress the evidence.

climax.text: Over 2,000 people were officially recognized as victims. The Minamata Convention now regulates mercury use worldwide.

punchline.text: Factory waste, fish, humans. Mercury climbed the food chain from bay to brain.

quiz.question: How did mercury poisoning spread through Minamata?
options: Methylmercury dumped in the bay accumulated in fish that residents ate
Mercury vapor from the factory was inhaled by nearby residents
Contaminated drinking water from factory runoff
